assumptions: This is the standard glibc `frame_dummy` CRT stub emitted by the compiler/linker; its body is identical to `register_tm_clones` (a tail call to it). The arithmetic on `completed.0` and the GOT load via `var0+0x3fe8` are the inlined body of register_tm_clones, not frame_dummy itself.

Replaced the entire decompiled body with a single call to `register_tm_clones()` because frame_dummy in C source is literally `static void frame_dummy(void) { register_tm_clones(); }` — the visible arithmetic comes from inlining at -O2.
Dropped the dead-store chain on `ret`/`arg1` (subtraction of equal values, shifts, zero-flag checks) — these are the inlined register_tm_clones prologue and not part of the original C source.
Function name in prototype is `sub_11e0` but the pseudocode header and role indicate this is `frame_dummy`; used the semantic name.
divergences: [low] other: The rewrite replaces the inlined body with a call to register_tm_clones(). This is a well-known CRT idiom; semantically equivalent assuming the inlined code is indeed register_tm_clones. The pseudocode shows the inlined version while the source shows the un-inlined call — behaviorally equivalent at the C-source level.

[low] control_flow: Pseudocode contains a trailing 'goto L_1160' after the return which appears to be unreachable/dead (likely decompiler artifact for alignment nop). Not represented in rewrite, but unreachable.
